# Impact Categories

The Connect document organizes impact under three H2 headings. Each heading represents a category of business impact. Use the descriptions below to determine which category an impact item belongs to.

---

## Deliver secure, high-quality content for customers

We are our customers' trusted way to learn about Microsoft technologies. As content developers, we deliver accurate, high-quality, and discoverable content ready for human and agentic consumption. That content helps customers solve problems, make informed decisions, and build confidently. We collaborate with subject matter experts across product and partner teams to ensure our content reflects product truth and customer signals. AI is embedded in our day-to-day workflows, enabling us to scale our efforts and maintain quality more efficiently than ever before. Throughout the content lifecycle, we uphold rigorous standards for quality and trustworthiness.

## Continuously improve and innovate

We continuously improve how we work to better meet our customers' needs and drive business impact. We drive measurable improvements to our operations and standards, pilot bold experiments to solve our business problems, and scale what works for greater results. We reflect on what we have learned, share learnings with each other, and partner whenever and wherever possible.

## Work better together with peers and partners

We collaborate in measurable and meaningful ways with our peers, partners, and stakeholders in support of Learn Content's FY26 focus areas. When working towards our team goals, we act with urgency, join in with others, adapt, and pivot quickly. We connect with each other, step in to help fill gaps, and share our knowledge in ways that can lead to impact for others and foster a "better together" culture.

---

> [!IMPORTANT]
> These category definitions change each fiscal year. Verify this file reflects the current year's org guidance before running the agent. Update this file if the definitions or names have changed.
